raw_response:
**Overall rating: TRUE**

The claim is correct: GitHub’s **“Block command line pushes that expose my email”** setting is what triggers the **GH007** push rejection when a pushed commit’s author email is one of your private GitHub emails. GitHub’s docs also make clear that this is tied to the private-email protection setting, not merely to whether an email address is individually visible in your profile.[1]

## Claim-by-claim analysis

| Claim | Rating | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| GitHub's setting “Block command line pushes that expose my email” causes the GH007 error “Your push would publish a private email address” when pushing commits that use a private email. | TRUE | GitHub says that when this setting is enabled, it checks the most recent commit and blocks the push if the author email is a private email on your GitHub account, warning that it would expose your private email. [1] |
| This is separate from each email's individual visibility flag. | TRUE | GitHub distinguishes between the **“Keep my email addresses private”** setting and the separate **“Block command line pushes that expose my email”** setting, which applies specifically to command-line pushes. [1] |

## Short summary

The statement is accurate and matches GitHub’s documentation closely: the GH007 error is caused by the command-line push privacy blocker when the commit author email is private, and it is distinct from the general email-visibility setting.[1]

## Source URLs

- https://docs.github.com/en/account-and-profile/how-tos/email-preferences/blocking-command-line-pushes-that-expose-your-personal-email-address
